data.title: Evonik Industries: 2Q Free Cash Flow of -EUR211M Due to Bonus Payouts, Temporary NWC Build-up
India's Indegene gains on quarterly profit rise
data.description:
** Shares of Indegene rise 5.2% to 571 rupees

** Stock logs biggest intraday pct gain since May 15

** Co posts a 32.7% Y/Y rise in June-qtr consol profit; consol rev from ops rises 12.4% Y/Y

** More than 1.6 mln shares change hands, 4.2x 30-day avg

** INEG rated "hold" by 5 analysts on average; median target price is 650 rupees – data compiled by LSEG

** Stock down 5.4% YTD

data.title: Australia's Althea Group falls on lower customer receipts
data.description:
** Shares of Althea Group fall as much as 3.9% to A$0.025, their lowest since July 3; stock last down 1.9% at A$0.0255

** Cannabis products maker records A$4.98 million ($3.20 million) in customer receipts for June-qtr, down from A$7.8 million last year

** Stock down 25% YTD, including moves in current session

($1 = 1.5540 Australian dollars)

data.title: Indian pharma stocks drop after Trump asks innovative drugmakers to cut prices in US
data.description:
** Indian pharma stocks drop 2.1%, top sectoral loser

** President Trump sent letters to leaders of 17 major drugmakers outlining how they should slash U.S. prescription drug prices to match those paid overseas

** While Indian drugmakers are not included in the list, it is a sentimental impact as price cuts by top innovative drugmakers could force smaller players to follow suit since it's a competitive industry, says Vishal Manchanda, pharma analyst at Systematix

** Nineteen of 20 stocks on sub-index in red, with losses led by SUN, which is down 3.5%

** "Sun Pharma is down the most because it has an exposure to innovative drugs and gets about 20% of its total sales from them," Manchanda says

** Cipla , Dr Reddy's decline 2.4% each

** YTD, NIPHARM down 4.8% vs Nifty 50's 4.7% climb

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 1 AM ET: Trump hits dozens more countries with higher tariffs that start Aug. 7 | Asia ...
data.description:
Trump hits dozens more countries with higher tariffs that start Aug. 7

President Donald Trump late Thursday stuck to his Friday deadline for hitting Canada with higher tariffs, but he gave a one-week delay to other trading partners that had received letters from him, while also announcing steeper levies for dozens of other countries.

----

Asia Stock Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific equity markets fell broadly, after President Trump announced steeper tariffs against dozens of countries while delaying the effective date by a week.

----

Tariffs, Economic Concerns Drag Down Stocks

The S&P 500 fell for the third straight session despite strong earnings from Meta and Microsoft.

----

Big Tech's $400 Billion AI Spending Spree Just Got Wall Street's Blessing

Microsoft joined Nvidia in the $4 trillion market-cap club, while Meta is closing in on the $2 trillion mark.

----

China's Manufacturing Activity Tumbles Into Contraction

A private gauge of China's manufacturing activity returned to contractionary territory in July, as softening new business growth led factories to scale back production.

----

South Korea's Exports Accelerate, Fueled by Chip Demand

Export growth was stronger than expected in July, driven by strong demand for high-performance computing chips amid an AI boom.

----

Stocks Keep Fighting a Hawkish Fed. Why That Has Worked.

Hawkish commentary from the central bank isn't derailing the market.

----

U.S.-Canada Trade Talks Dealing With Aluminum-Tariff Levels, Bessent Says

The Treasury secretary's comments signaled possible relief for Canada's aluminum sector.

----

Trump's Tariffs Questioned by Appeals Court

Judges voice skepticism that the president can use emergency powers to replace the import schedule Congress adopted.

----

Canadian Economy Seen Picking Up Slightly After Contracting in May

National statistics agency's advance estimate suggests the economy grew just 0.1% on an annualized basis in the second quarter.

----

Trump's Russia Sanctions Strategy Runs Hot and Cold

The president's tighter deadline to reach peace with Ukraine and his threat against Russia's energy customers contrast with a lighter approach to Russia elsewhere.

----

Core PCE Inflation Runs a Touch Hot, Dimming Hopes for September Rate Cut

The Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the personal consumption expenditures price index, is due on Thursday.

----

Jobless claims stay low as layoff activity remains subdued

The data indicate a stable labor market with low layoff activity.

----

EU Expects Spirits, Wine to Be Hit With 15% U.S. Tariff

The U.S. and EU are ironing out the finer details of their trade agreement. Wine and sprits are not expected to be exempted from U.S. tariffs, according to an EU official.

----

Major Banks Nudge Oil Forecast Higher But U.S. Tariffs, OPEC+ Hikes Weigh on Outlook

Major Wall Street banks have modestly lifted their oil-price forecasts for this year and next, but lingering uncertainty over U.S. tariffs and rising OPEC+ output continue to cloud the outlook.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 1 AM ET: Apple's iPhone Sales Blow Past Estimates as Customers Raced to Beat Tariffs | Amazon ...
data.description:
Apple's iPhone Sales Blow Past Estimates as Customers Raced to Beat Tariffs

The company's revenue was about 10% higher than the same period a year ago, showing an unexpected ability to weather tariff blows.

----

Amazon Shares Fall Because Cloud Unit's Growth Wasn't Enough for Wall Street

The e-commerce giant's quarterly sales results beat expectations, but shares dropped 7% because its cloud computing arm's growth wasn't as strong as rivals'.

----

Why Ford's Made-in-America Strategy Hurts It in Trump's Trade War

The company says new tariff deals with Japan, the EU and South Korea put it at a disadvantage.

----

Berkshire Stock Sees Weird Trading. The A Shares Pop While B Shares Dip.

It is unclear what caused the late-day buying in the A shares, but it looks like it will reverse Friday.

----

Axiata to Start Sale Process For World's Sixth-Largest Tower Company

Malaysian telecom giant Axiata Group is expected to start the sale process this month to sell a majority stake in the world's sixth-largest telecommunications tower company, people familiar with the process said.

----

Bayer Ups Guidance After Pharmaceuticals Unit Outperforms

The German pharmaceutical and agricultural conglomerate said it now expects to post revenue of more than $52.51 billion for the year.

----

Wells Fargo to Name CEO Charlie Scharf Chairman

The appointment rewards the executive who led the bank back from a scandal involving the creation of millions of fake customer accounts.

----

UnitedHealth Group Replaces CFO John Rex

The insurance giant names Wayne DeVeydt as its new finance chief amid a turnaround effort.

----

Trump Talks Tough on Drug Prices but His Demands Have Softened

The president threatened to "deploy every tool" in the administration's arsenal if drug companies don't cut certain prices by late September.

----

Coinbase's Profit Jumps on Crypto Investment Gains

The largest U.S. crypto exchange's revenue fell short of Wall Street expectations.

----

Chevron Is On a Roll Ahead of Second-Quarter Earnings

The energy explorer just closed the acquisition of Hess, and got permission from the Trump administration to resume operations in Venezuela.

----

Paramount Streaming Revenue Grows, While TV Challenges Continue

The media company is set to merge with Skydance Global next week.

----

Reddit Swings to Second-Quarter Profit as AI-Driven Improvements Boost Ad Revenue

Reddit's AI-driven advertisements have helped the company draw in more business, pushing revenue up 78% to $499.6 million, said CFO Drew Vollero.

----

MicroStrategy Swings to Profit on Bitcoin Gains

The company's bitcoin per share increased by 25% in the second quarter from a year earlier.

----

Figma Shares Jump 250% in Their Stock-Market Debut

The software company adds fuel to an IPO comeback already under way.

data.title: Evonik Industries 2Q EPS EUR0.26
Analysts Had Seen Evonik Industries 2Q EPS EUR0.30
Evonik Outlook For 2025: Adjusted EBITDA To Reach Lower End Of Projected Range
data.description:
EVONIK INDUSTRIES AG :

  • FY ADJUSTED EBITDA EUR 2,000-2,300 MILLION

  • ADJUSTED EBITDA DECLINES BY 12 PERCENT IN Q2

  • Q2 NET INCOME AMOUNTED TO EUR 120 MILLION, COMPARED WITH EUR -5 MILLION IN PRIOR-YEAR QUARTER

  • Q2 ADJUSTED EBITDA MARGIN AT 14.5 PERCENT REMAINED AT ABOUT LEVEL OF LAST YEAR (Q2 2024: 14.7 PERCENT)

  • QUARTERLY REVENUE WAS EUR 3.50 BILLION, 11 PERCENT LOWER YEAR-OVER-YEAR (Q2 2024: EUR 3.93 BILLION)

  • OUTLOOK FOR 2025: ADJUSTED EBITDA TO REACH LOWER END OF PROJECTED RANGE

  • STILL EXPECTS CASH CONVERSION RATE TO BE AROUND 40 PERCENT IN 2025

  • OUTLOOK 2025: RETURN ON CAPITAL EMPLOYED IS NOW EXPECTED TO BE IN LINE WITH PREVIOUS YEAR'S LEVEL (2024: 7.1 PERCENT)

  • CAPITAL EXPENDITURES WILL BE REDUCED BY EUR 100 MILLION TO ABOUT EUR 750 MILLION IN 2025

data.title: Fugro lowers full-year core profit margin
data.description:
Dutch geological data specialist Fugro on Friday lowered its core profit margin outlook for the year.

Fugro, which provies geotechnical, survey, subsea and geosciences services, forecast an earnings before interest, taxes margin of between 8% and 11%.

The group previously forecast an annual EBIT margin target of between 11% and 15%.

data.title: Evonik Industries: Unfavorable USD Rates Dragged on Results
Evonik Industries: Results Reflect Tougher Macro Environment in May, June
Australia's Origin Energy rises, brokerages highlight upbeat outlook
data.description:
** Shares of oil and gas retailer Origin Energy rise 1.2% to A$11.81

** Jarden upgrades rating to "Neutral" from "Underweight" and raises price target to A$11.30 from A$10.95

** Brokerage says ORG's qtrly results indicate a decline in upstream production as the Australia Pacific LNG project grapples with strategies to restore output to prior levels

** However, as Jarden's forecasts already account for increased capex and reduced production, the emphasis is on strong momentum developing in energy markets division

** UBS says ORG's "solid" quarterly results de-risk Australia Pacific LNG project production and costs into the medium term

** Stock down ~2% this week, set for its worst week since June 16

data.title: UK Stocks-Factors to watch on August 1
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening lower on Friday, with futures down 0.2%.

* MOTOR FINANCE: The UK's Supreme Court will announce a long-awaited ruling on car finance commissions on Friday that could influence whether major banks face a multibillion-pound compensation bill.

* STEEL: Britain said the European Union will remove tariffs on key steel products under a quota system from Friday as part of a reset of ties and a recent deal to ease trade barriers.

* COMMODITIES: Oil prices were little changed, gold prices traded flat, and copper prices were mixed on Friday.

* FTSE: British equities closed mixed on Thursday, as investors assessed a spate of corporate earnings and looked for potential trade deals before the August 1 tariff deadline expires.

* UK CORPORATE DIARY:

ICAG

Q2 results

Pearson

HY results

Melrose Industries

HY results

* For more on the factors affecting European stocks, please click on:

data.title: Fresenius Medical Care AG expected to post earnings of 47 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Fresenius Medical Care AG , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Bad Homburg Vor Der Hohe Hessen-based company is expected to report a 0.9% increase in revenue to €4.811 billion from €4.77 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Fresenius Medical Care AG is for earnings of 47 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Fresenius Medical Care AG is $30.00, about 15.5% above its last closing price of $25.34
